steps.title: Identify the verb's subject
steps.content: In the result clause—“that her book's musings on tenth-century Japanese courtly life ______ readers a thousand years later”—the subject is musings, a plural noun.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: subject-verb agreement. The subject is "her book's musings on tenth-century Japanese courtly life" — the head noun is the plural "musings." Rule: the verb agrees with the head noun of the subject phrase, not with a nearby singular noun.
  • Choice A — has fascinated: Singular perfect. Treats "musings" as singular — agreement error. ✗
  • Choice B — fascinates: Singular simple present. Same agreement error. ✗
  • Choice C — fascinate: Plural simple present. Correctly agrees with the plural head noun "musings." ✓
  • Choice D — is fascinating: Singular progressive. Agreement error and unnecessary progressive aspect. ✗
That matches Choice C — fascinate.
